"'''A Day in the Life'''" was a short comic in [[Star Wars Tales 12|''Star Wars Tales'' 12]] and was collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 3]]'' and ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The New Republic Vol. 2]]''. The story was written by [[Brett Matthews]], colors by [[Guy Major]] and letters by [[Steve Dutro]]. It takes place three days after the Battle of Endor.

==Plot summary==
A small patrol of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] from [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], led by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] encounter the still-deadly remains of the downed [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. After losing one of their [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], the surviving pair come to terms with the loss and Antilles writes the letter to the dead pilot's next of kin.

==Continuity==
''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' includes individual entries for the characters of [[Kirst (pilot)|Kirst]] and [[Wister]], which serves to endorse the [[canon]]icity of this ''Tales'' story.

Nevertheless, the story is stated to take place three days after the Battle of Endor, in which Wedge Antilles, in command of Rogue Squadron, and Luke Skywalker are still stationed with the Alliance Fleet in orbit above Endor. This time frame is in direct contradiction with the events taking place in the novel ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'', in which Skywalker, Antilles, and Rogue Squadron depart from Endor two days after the battle there to assist Imperial forces on the planet [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]]. The novel reveals that it would take "days" to travel from Endor to Bakura; thus, it stands that Skywalker, Antilles, and Rogue Squadron are in hyperspace transit three days after the Battle of Endor.
